transcript: Think of and you can start now. Cat, dog, mouse, horse, zebra, elephant. When I get to the zoo. Rhinoceros, snake, lion, tiger, jaguar, snake, deer, rabbit, fox. Good grief. Giraffe, rhinoceros, hippopotamus, monkey. Okay, that's the letter F. All the words you can think of that begin with the letter F and you can start now. Farm, fun, feeling. Good grief. Any word at all. Can't even think of F letters. Funny, fin, flower, fox.
patient_only_transcript: Cat, dog, mouse, horse, zebra, elephant. When I get to the zoo. Rhinoceros, snake, lion, tiger, jaguar, snake, deer, rabbit, fox. Good grief. Giraffe, rhinoceros, hippopotamus, monkey. Okay, that's the letter F. Farm, fun, feeling. Good grief. Any word at all. Can't even think of F letters. Funny, fin, flower, fox.
speaker_turn_summary: Examiner: 'Think of and you can start now. Cat, dog, mouse, horse, zebra, elephant.'
Patient: 'When I get to the zoo.'
Patient: 'Rhinoceros, snake, lion, tiger, jaguar, snake, deer, rabbit, fox.'
Patient: 'Good grief.'
Patient: 'Giraffe, rhinoceros, hippopotamus, monkey.'
Examiner: 'Okay, that's the letter F. All the words you can think of that begin with the letter F and you can start now.'
Patient: 'Farm, fun, feeling.'
Patient: 'Good grief.'
Patient: 'Any word at all. Can't even think of F letters.'
Patient: 'Funny, fin, flower, fox.'
patient_cue_summary: Repeated pauses and fillers ('Good grief', 'Can't even think of F letters')
Partial and fragmented fluency responses with repetition ('snake' listed twice)
Inconsistent task adherence: switches from animal list to F-words with examiner prompt
Limited lexical retrieval in F-word task: only 5 words produced, with hesitation and self-correction
Examiner-dependent continuation: patient explicitly states inability to generate F-words without prompting

voice_findings.label: vocal_frustration
voice_findings.summary: Patient vocalized 'Good grief' and 'Can't even think of F letters' with audible exasperation
voice_findings.evidence: Patient explicitly stated frustration and cognitive difficulty during F-word task

voice_findings.label: speech_hesitation
voice_findings.summary: Frequent pauses, fillers, and self-corrections during fluency tasks
voice_findings.evidence: Multiple pauses, 'uh', 'good grief', and restarting of utterances observed in audio

content_findings.label: fluency_task_performance
content_findings.summary: Patient generated animal names with repetition and incomplete sequencing; struggled with F-word task
content_findings.evidence: Listed animals with duplicate 'snake'; produced only 5 F-words with hesitation and self-admission of difficulty

content_findings.label: task_adherence
content_findings.summary: Patient completed animal list but showed reduced engagement and dependence on examiner during F-word task
content_findings.evidence: Switched from animal task to F-word task only after examiner prompt; explicitly stated inability to generate F-words

assessment.screening_summary: Patient demonstrates mild fluency impairment during verbal fluency tasks, including pauses, repetition, and explicit self-reported difficulty with word retrieval. Task-switching and examiner dependence suggest possible early cognitive decline. Further clinical evaluation is recommended.
evidence_for_risk: Repeated pauses and fillers during fluency tasks
Self-reported inability to generate F-words without prompting
Fragmented and repetitive animal list with incomplete sequencing
Emotional cues indicating frustration and cognitive effort
evidence_against_risk: Patient correctly generated multiple animal names and some F-words
No overt disorientation or loss of context
Task completion was attempted despite difficulty
alternative_explanations: Temporary cognitive load or stress during testing
Mild language processing delay without dementia-level impairment
risk_factor_findings: Self-reported difficulty with word retrieval under task pressure
Task-switching with observable hesitation and frustration
subjective_assessment_findings: Patient explicitly states 'Can't even think of F letters' and 'Good grief' indicating frustration
emotional_assessment_findings: Frustration and mild distress evident in vocal tone and self-reported statements
Reduced engagement in F-word task with visible cognitive effort and pause markers
risk_control_suggestions: Repeat verbal fluency task with different categories or longer time limits
Administer formal cognitive screening battery (e.g., MoCA, MMSE)
Assess functional impact and caregiver report in follow-up
assessment.visit_recommendation: Prompt clinical evaluation with formal cognitive screening and functional assessment
assessment.future_risk_trend_summary: Longitudinal prediction is limited based on single clip; follow-up is recommended to monitor progression or rule out transient factors
context_notes: Audio-only assessment with no visual cues available
Patient speech is partially intelligible but shows impairment-like cues
patient responses are the primary evaluation focus
assessment.disclaimer: Research demo only. This output is not a diagnosis and must not be used as a standalone medical decision.
